posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked: 1988 Kalin Cellars Cuvee Rose Reserve, California Sparkling Wine. I purchased this recently from an absolutely pristine cellar where it had been stored, untouched, since release. So after the recent praise on the Blanc from the one and only @William_Kelley , needless to say I was pretty damn excited to open this. As always, my guy Mr. Kelley was spot on. This was absolutely incredible! As in…one of the better sparkling wines I’ve had in a long time. Beautiful pale salmon color with plenty or bubbles (see the 3rd pic/video) and a great nose filled with yeasty citrus and strawberries. In the mouth this is just amazing. Vinuous, energetic, and very fresh with more strawberries, baked bread, and some slightly bitter minerality on the very long finish. Blind, you would be hard pressed to clock this as a 35 year old sparkling wine from CA. A total stunner and a contender for one of my top wines of the year. Life is good!

posts.name: Robert Alfert

The Semillion is outstanding. Amazing how these wines age.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
From the Lorenzo vineyard in Russian River, before Helen Turley took the fruit for Marcassin (where it was always their best Chardonnay IMO). Ten years sur lattes, then disgorged with zero dosage.

They made a few different sparkling wines, but not all were disgorged and the crown caps they used for tirage have sadly deteriorated pretty badly.

posts.name: Mike_M3y3rs
posts.cooked:
Fascinating to see this. +1 that wasn’t aware of the sparking. These are such interesting wines.

Curious if anyone has yet seen the previously unreleased vintages show up that William alluded to in his some of his written remarks upon Terry Leighton’s death earlier this year?

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: They disgorged and released three, as far as I know: this 1988 Cuvée Blanche, a rosé of the same year, and a 1987 rosé (which I have never tasted). John Gilman has some notes/commentary on them in his database.

posts.name: David Milner (ITB)
